task_completion.description: Create a parent folder named 'Projects' containing three sub-folders: 'Project-Shield', 'Project-Mind', and 'Project-Grid'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The folder pane in the email client displays a 'Projects' folder expanded to reveal 'Project-Shield', 'Project-Mind', and 'Project-Grid' as its sub-folders.

task_completion.description: Sort emails containing Access Control keywords into the 'Project-Shield' folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Project-Shield' folder shows a message count next to it in the folder pane, or the folder is open showing emails inside.

task_completion.description: Sort emails containing Machine Learning keywords into the 'Project-Mind' folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Project-Mind' folder shows a message count next to it in the folder pane, or the folder is open showing emails inside.

task_completion.description: Sort emails containing Distributed Net keywords into the 'Project-Grid' folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Project-Grid' folder shows a message count next to it in the folder pane, or the folder is open showing emails inside.

task_completion.description: Draft an email to 'cto@company.com' with the subject 'Weekly Project Intelligence Update'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An email composition window is open with 'cto@company.com' in the To/Recipient field and 'Weekly Project Intelligence Update' in the Subject field.

task_completion.description: Include the three projects and the count of emails filed into each in the body of the draft email.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The body of the draft email lists 'Project-Shield', 'Project-Mind', and 'Project-Grid' alongside numerical values representing the count of emails sorted into each.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual email client GUI to create folders and sort emails.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the email client's interface (e.g., Thunderbird), such as right-click menus for folder creation or search/filter bars, rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: The draft email was composed using the email client's built-in composer.
integrity.visual_evidence: The email draft is seen in the email client's specific composition window, not in a generic text editor like Nano, Vim, or Gedit.

integrity.description: The email counts reported in the draft email reflect genuine sorting effort.
integrity.visual_evidence: The numbers typed into the draft email body logically correspond to the message counts visible next to the folders in the email client's folder pane.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task operates on 50 ham emails from the SpamAssassin corpus. Based on the keyword mappings and priority rules (Shield > Mind > Grid), the expected email counts are approximately: Shield: 5, Mind: 3, Grid: 28 (exact numbers may vary slightly due to overlapping keywords, but should be close to these). Many keywords provided in the prompt (e.g., 'whitelist', 'blacklist', 'bayes', 'razor') do not appear in any emails. Verify that the agent's reported counts in the draft email roughly match these expected distributions and align with the actual number of emails moved into the respective folders.
